title: Living in Boulder blog 8 - 2 - 2017: Clement of Alexandria

enrichment.summary: Kyle recounts spending an entire day re-reading Clement of Alexandria's thousand-page work only to discover he had already read it, due to a folder organization mix-up with his Boulder reading pile. He reflects on the wasted day, his drive to push through and finish the church fathers, and briefly recommends the church fathers as prolific, opinionated writers for anyone interested in Christianity.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.topic: wasting a day on duplicate reading
stances.position: It was a big bummer but he didn't freak out; it made him want to double down and work harder
Highly recommends them — they're prolific, opinionated on everything, and you'll never run out of material
Motivated to complete them so he can move on; sees it as nearly done and very exciting

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Moving in Boulder is scheduled for August 2nd.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He worked all day on 'Climbing of Alexander' and completed a thousand-page book.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He realized he had already done the same work, leading to a wasted day.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He had mixed up his completed and uncompleted books, causing the duplication.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: This was the biggest reading volume he has ever done in one day.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He hasn't wasted a day like this in a while and usually has a good track record.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The mistake was a big bummer but made him want to work even harder.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He plans to do a little more work tonight because he feels close to finishing.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Many of the books he's working on have a lot of writing, but he's powering through.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He mentions being totally absorbed in his work, referencing 'nobias, tias, commandias, directory, thou, my two, the belated, 6.0, the l.s. ever kind'.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He is prepared to work all week to finish his current project.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Once done, he will have read almost everything except some fragmented poets.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He is excited to move on to other projects after finishing his current work.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He was listening to TASA's owls and a play by a church father.

claims.subject: church fathers
claims.claim: Church fathers who lived around the fall of Rome were prolific writers.

claims.subject: church fathers
claims.claim: They wrote instruction manuals and had opinions about everything, including shoes.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He recommends listening to church fathers for those interested in Christianity and the Bible.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: There is an endless amount of material to listen to from the church fathers.

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder, V-Log, for, I guess, August 2nd, it's August 3rd, 2, 14 a.m.
Right now, um, so, I, uh, got out, did some work, and then came back, and spent, like,
All day long working on, um, Clement of Alexandria, and I did a thousand-page book, and then, uh,
Like, uh, I guess a bunch of books, but then I realized that I already did it, I doubled
Up, on a thousand-page book, and then a 300-page book, 1,300 pages, what happened was, I had
All my used ones, I mean, the ones that I've done in a folder, and then I had this folder
For the ones that I hadn't done yet, which were just empty, um, and, um, and somehow, I had
Some books that were already done, were also in the empty folder, it's empty, so, um, it
Like, after I spent all day long, I was patting myself on the back from doing a thousand-page
Book with, like, a hundred chapters or something, and, um, um, and, you know, doing it all in
One day, and only to find out that the whole day was lost, because I already did it, but
That's the biggest, um, I think in terms of volume, that's the biggest redo that I've ever
Done, um, and I haven't wasted a day in a while, so, I think for that kind of stuff, I have a pretty
Good track record, so it wasn't really, like, I was freaking out, but it was a bummer, big
Bummer, um, and it made me, like, double down, and want to go even harder, which is kind of
Stressful, I was doing that a little bit tonight, um, because I feel like I'm almost done, all
I have to do is, but, you know, a lot of these guys have a lot of writing, but even if with
A lot of writing, I can zoom right through it, but, um, you know, like Anatolias, Arnobius,
Caius, Comandius, Gregory, Thaumaturges, Hippolytus, Exeotio, Julius Evercranus, anyways,
There's just, like, ten of them, maybe I can do them all tomorrow, but I'm prepared to go
All this week on it, you know, because once I'm done, I have all, I have everything, pretty
Much, you know, except for, like, the little, like, poets who have, like, a fraction, a fragment
Here, a fragment there, you know, I pretty much have everything, so, um, yeah, then I
Can link it on my education page and back out and do other stuff, super exciting, that's
Why I was, like, I'm so, um, I'm working so hard right now to try to get it done, but,
Um, whatever, what is, I was listening to Tacitus Annals, and then I went and I was listening
To, uh, Plato Republic, and then one of these Church Father guys, which are really interesting,
Because these Church Father dudes who write so much, and there's lots of them, they've
Lived right at the fall of Rome, man, they're prolific, they have, and it's like they're,
They have instruction manuals about everything, they have opinions about everything, like shoes,
You know, everything, like you name it, it's crazy, so, yeah, if you're into, like, Christianity
And the Bible, you should listen to the Church Fathers on my website, um, you'll never run
Out of stuff to listen to. Okay, anyways, I think that's enough talking for now. Peace out.
